title: Short-lived tokens with revocation
description: Access tokens are short-lived and revocable; long sessions rely on refresh with server-side revocation, not on long token TTLs.
body: Use when changing session length, token TTL, or refresh logic. Do not extend an access-token TTL to avoid re-auth friction. Actions: keep access-token lifetime short; implement refresh with a server-side revocation list or rotating refresh tokens; invalidate on logout and on credential change. Validation signals include a test that a revoked/expired token is rejected and that logout invalidates the session. Risk boundary: lengthening token life or removing revocation is a permission-expansion change and needs a human gate.
